policy.reason: These commits are durable, focused-tested engineering evidence. They remain unpromoted and do not collectively satisfy complete persistence coverage, the required retail differentials, promoted-binary provenance, or the clean-install certification package.

slices.subject: Support persistent Fallout containers
limitations: No whole-corpus container differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Recover Fallout furniture lifecycle state
limitations: No complete furniture/package corpus differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout actor value metadata
limitations: Metadata is not complete actor-value evaluation
No mutation/persistence or retail differential

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout faction records
limitations: Typed records are not reputation/disguise/crime runtime behavior
No persistence or retail differential

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout NPC runtime state
limitations: Existing compatibility stat shell is not a complete native FNV mechanics model
No mutable player-state persistence or retail differential

slices.subject: Decode Fallout SCDA instruction frames
limitations: Instruction framing is not a complete GECK VM or event scheduler
No opcode-wide retail differential

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout creature runtime state
limitations: Template-category inheritance remains incomplete
Creature skills/type/base damage and extended AI fields are not consumed
No persistence or retail differential

slices.subject: Resolve native Fallout player state
scope: fourteen skill values and raw offsets
limitations: This is immutable authored/base state, not mutable runtime actor values
No native SPECIAL/skill mechanics consumption or save round-trip
No perks, limbs, appearance, or inventory state

slices.subject: Execute bounded Fallout quest bytecode
limitations: Only two compiled opcodes execute natively
Unsupported opcodes retain temporary whole-SCTX fallback
No general object/dialogue/event VM coverage

slices.subject: Resolve Fallout creature template categories
scope: validated direct and LVLC bridge edges
runtime stats, AI, package, model, movement, scale, and name routing
limitations: 541 LVLC-backed templates still use deterministic level-1 selection
Traits, factions, effects, inventory, and script category consumers remain incomplete
No retail differential or persistence certification

slices.subject: Align Fallout moon regression with retail window
limitations: Test-only correction; no runtime behavior changed
No new live retail differential
No certification credit

slices.subject: Execute core Fallout quest state bytecode
limitations: Only six of 112 observed QUST-stage opcode identities execute in this slice
3,573 of 5,082 winning-live frames remain outside the recognized command set
Mixed stages still use temporary whole-SCTX fallback
Recursive SetStage coverage is tracked separately
No retail differential or promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Persist mutable Fallout player actor values
scope: immutable raw DNAM offset provenance validation
limitations: Single current-value layer is not the retail actor-value modifier stack
No invented retail clamps
Raw skill offsets are preserved but not interpreted
No proxy-health sync because DATA base/max/current semantics remain unproven
No production GetActorValue consumer yet
No retail differential or promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Populate Fallout creature fixed inventory
scope: safe duplicate-stack merge and overflow rejection
clone refnum refresh and mutation isolation
limitations: The subsequent deterministic-fallout-leveled-inventory slice resolves only 110 of 1,606 effective official creature LVLI uses; chance, global, COED, random, PCLevelMult, ambiguous, and unsupported paths remain fail-closed
Mutable creature-state persistence is implemented only by the subsequent persistent-fallout-creature-state slice
No whole-corpus inventory differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Execute transactional Fallout SetStage bytecode
scope: SetStage 0x1039 with exact r/n argument grammar
cycle detection and explicit 32-stage recursion cap
limitations: Strict root-plus-target purity makes only 6 of 148 winning-live authored SetStage frames executable across five root stages
The bounded startup-spine set gains no executable frame
Unsupported conditions and any source or opcode fallback reject the complete touched graph
3,573 of 5,082 winning-live quest frames remain outside the recognized command set
No retail differential or promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Persist mutable Fallout creature state
scope: explicit non-FNV ESM4 omission and rejection
limitations: CreatureStats SPECIAL, AI, effects, and spells remain a compatibility persistence shell rather than native FNV actor-value parity
FNV base effects deliberately map to an empty compatibility SpellList
Unaccessed unchanged FNV creatures remain omitted
Duplicate suppression is scoped to one CellStore read pass
Validation is not an exhaustive hostile or obsolete-save semantic guarantee
No whole-corpus creature-state differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout New Vegas perk records
scope: 259 physical and 257 winning-live official PERK records
strict FNV subrecord order and payload sizes
embedded script byte and source preservation
limitations: Parser and typed store only
No perk condition evaluation or effect execution
No perk UI, mutation, or persistence behavior
No whole-corpus runtime differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Resolve deterministic Fallout leveled inventory
scope: strict FNV LVLI grammar and payload sizes
nested deterministic use-all and calc-each expansion
complete plan and existing-stack overflow preflight
mutable CSTA round-trip of resolved inventory
limitations: Official deterministic gain is 110 of 1,606 effective creature LVLI root/reference uses (6.8493 percent), or 110 of 1,544 fixed-level uses (7.1244 percent)
Only 18 of 829 entire creature roots are fully deterministic
Chance-none, LVLG globals, COED, random ambiguity, PCLevelMult, unknown flags, unsupported terminal types, overflow, cycles, and depth above 16 fail closed
CLOT, INGR, and LIGH remain direct-inventory-only because no official deterministic LVLI target proves them
No whole-corpus runtime differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Persist mutable Fallout NPC state
scope: FNV-only NPC_/ACHR CSTA read and write
outer reference count, scale, position, enabled, and RefNum state
compatibility CreatureStats health, death, and AI sequence state
OBJE and base-record kind rejection before typed payload loading
explicitly empty saved AI sequence preservation
limitations: The frozen official denominator is 3,942 winning-live ACHR placements, but no whole-corpus per-placement differential has run
CreatureStats is a compatibility persistence shell rather than native FNV actor-value, skill, perk, or effect semantics
No equipped visual slots, furniture lifecycle, ragdoll, ACHR scripts, respawn or leveled-NPC behavior
Unaccessed unchanged FNV NPCs remain omitted
Duplicate suppression is scoped to one CellStore read pass
Validation is not an exhaustive hostile or obsolete-save semantic guarantee
No retail differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Honor authored Fallout door keys
scope: locked FNV door activation checks the activating actor inventory
authored key unlock without consuming the key
keyed load door continues to ActionTeleport
keyed ordinary door continues to ActionDoor
missing actor and wrong or missing key remain locked with FailedAction
125 of 341 authored locked door placements have a valid live KEYM
44 of 97 authored locked load doors have a valid live KEYM
limitations: The direct corpus reach is conditional on the actor actually carrying the authored key
The remaining 216 keyless locks need lockpicking or script behavior
No mutable ESM4 door persistence
No ordinary-door close visibility fix
No door script, faction crime, NoAccess, enable-parent, XTEL flag, or ignored base-flag behavior
Only 2 of 1,378 load-door transitions have bounded observations and zero are accepted
No natural journey or retail differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Parse exact Fallout New Vegas terminal records
scope: 515 winning-live official TERM records and 1,350 menu items
strict FNV subrecord grammar, ordering, payload sizes, duplicate and truncation rejection
top-level identity, name, text, model, script, password and sound fields
RNAM result text without overwrite loss
INAM note/display and TNAM submenu links
SCHR, SCDA, SCTX, SLSD, SCVR, SCRV and SCRO embedded-script preservation
exact raw byte and source preservation
FormId adjustment including FNV condition parameters
limitations: Parser only and therefore no terminal gameplay parity credit
No terminal activation or UI
No hacking, lockout, sound, condition evaluation or embedded-script execution
No note/submenu mutation behavior
No terminal persistence
No natural terminal journey or retail differential
No promoted-binary provenance

slices.subject: Materialize Fallout New Vegas talking activators
scope: all 116 winning-live official REFR-to-TACT placements materialize across 75 used bases
dedicated TACT runtime class with name, model, tooltip, activator identity and Lua type
deterministic zero initialization for absent XRDO data
all 29 RadioStation placements retain exact XRDO data
authored SNAM loop takes precedence over INAM template fallback
15 of 29 placed radio stations have a deterministic direct sound action
87 non-radio TACT placements materialize without radio misclassification
malformed and missing references fail closed
limitations: Direct-sound coverage is 15 of 116 total TACT placements, or 15 of 29 RadioStation placements
Fourteen placed RadioStation TACT references have neither SNAM nor INAM and remain NullAction
No DIAL/INFO sequencing, conditions, clock, randomization, Pip-Boy, range, static or audio-location behavior
No TACT script, dialogue, animation-sequence or station-state persistence
Sound action construction is tested but playback execution has no retail differential
No whole-corpus live journey
No promoted-binary provenance
No parity credit

slices.subject: Parse and store Fallout New Vegas recipes
scope: strict FNV subrecord grammar, ordering, payload sizes, duplicate and truncation rejection
signed skill actor value and required skill level
limitations: Parser and typed store only
No workbench activation or crafting UI
No skill, condition, ingredient, output, or quantity runtime evaluation
No inventory consumption or crafted-output mutation
RCCT DATA remains deliberately opaque
No crafting persistence, natural journey, or retail differential
No promoted-binary provenance
No parity credit

slices.subject: Persist mutable Fallout door state
scope: ESM4 DOOR references participate in generic ObjectState cell save and load
mutable count, scale, position, enabled, and unlocked state round-trip
immutable XTEL destination door, cell, and transform retention
finite outer-state, non-negative count, and set RefNum prevalidation
OBJE and base-record kind rejection before typed state loading
headless WorldModel load retains data state while optional scene and physics notifications remain runtime-only
limitations: ESM3 save serialization canonicalizes an unlocked door lock level to zero while preserving unlocked state
No moving-door animation or open/closing DoorState persistence
No ordinary-door close visibility fix
No lockpicking, trap, script, faction crime, NoAccess, enable-parent, XTEL-flag, or ignored base-flag behavior
Unchanged authored doors remain omitted
Duplicate suppression is scoped to one CellStore read pass
Validation is not an exhaustive hostile or obsolete-save semantic guarantee
No whole-corpus door-state differential or natural journey
No promoted-binary provenance
No parity credit

slices.subject: Run strict read-only Fallout terminals
scope: FNV-only dedicated TERM runtime class and activation
complete immutable preflight before any UI
exact strict predicate reaches 3 of 515 winning-live TERM records, 3 of 357 placed terminal references, and 3 of 1,350 menu items
two accepted DNAM byte shapes preserved as opaque contracts
strict required identity, bounds, model, name, description, menu text, and result text
DESC to ITXT selection to RNAM blocking presentation
ANAM zero completion and ANAM two bounded redraw
immutable prepared-session runner with no game-state mutator
null, wrong-type, deleted, non-FNV, malformed, conditional, scripted, note, submenu, sound, password, and unsupported top-field paths fail closed
limitations: Runtime coverage is only 3 of 1,350 official menu items, 3 of 515 TERM records, and 3 of 357 placed references
Blocking generic message boxes are not retail terminal visuals, layout, input, timing, camera, animation, or sound
DNAM byte meanings are deliberately not inferred, so hacking, lock, lockout, and difficulty behavior are absent
No CTDA evaluation, embedded or top-level script execution, INAM note display, TNAM submenu traversal, sound, password, mutation, custom state, or CSTA persistence
ANAM two permits sixteen completed redraws; a seventeenth would-be redraw stops only after its description and result UI were shown
No natural terminal journey or retail differential
No promoted-binary provenance
No certified or retail visual parity credit
